item1.entityHistory: Originally Florida corporation formed July 29, 2002, converted to LLC October 18, 2021
item1.businessDescription: Area developer franchise for youth sports leagues, camps, tournaments, clinics, training, development, social activities, special events, and related products/services

item3.unresolvedNote: Item 3 content not fully surfaced — hasLitigation from prior pass

item4.priorPassNote: Prior lightweight extraction marked hasBankruptcy=true, corrected based on 4-pass review — no bankruptcy disclosure found in surfaced text

tenYear.territoryBasis: up to 79,999 children age 14 and under

feeStructure.existingFranchiseeDiscount: 10% off then-current franchise fee for additional 10-year franchise

item6.royaltyFormula: Greater of 7.5% of Network Revenues or monthly minimum based on territory child population

localAdvertisingRequirement.rate: 2% of Network Revenues per month in network area
localAdvertisingRequirement.deficiencyConsequence: Franchisor may require deficiency paid into national fund

technologyFee.futureOption1: up to 2% of Network Revenues per month

networkRevenuesDefinition.centralizedBillingNote: Franchisor may invoice customers directly, remitting net amounts after deducting royalty, fund contributions, CSC fee, credit card fees, insurance fees, and other amounts

otherFees.name: Transfer fee (10-year)
otherFees.amount: greater of 50% of then-current franchise fee or $12,450

otherFees.name: Late payment interest
otherFees.amount: lesser of 18%/year or highest lawful rate

liquidatedDamages.tenYear: average monthly royalty from prior 12 months x lesser of 24 months or remaining term
liquidatedDamages.fiveYear: remaining months x $500 monthly franchise fee
liquidatedDamages.note: Severe — effectively accelerates future royalties on early termination

item8.dropShipNote: Orders placed through online ordering system, drop shipped directly to franchisee from approved supplier
item8.centralizedBillingNote: Franchisor controls billing system, payment rails, and may invoice customers directly
item8.unresolvedNote: Full Item 8 table not surfaced — partial from agreement snippets and post-opening obligations

item9.unresolvedNote: Matrix referenced but not fully surfaced

item11.keyManagerNote: Cannot serve more than one i9 Sports franchise
item11.twoMilestonesNote: Business Launch Date (can market/sell) and Service Start Date (can conduct events) are different

annualConference.attendees: Operating Principal (1 franchise) or OP + Key Manager per franchise (multiple)

ongoingSupport: Billing/customer account coordination via Billing System

item12.territoryBasis: geography and child population (up to 79,999 children age 14 and under)
item12.territoryDefinition: contiguous geographic area defined by metro boundaries, streets, zip codes, subdivisions, or other criteria
item12.protectionScope: Neither franchisor nor affiliates will open or grant another i9 Sports franchise in network area during term while compliant
item12.noModificationException: technical adjustments like zip code changes
reservedRights: Solicit customers in network area even if franchisee already serves them
Distribute products/services through wholesale, retail, internet, telemarketing, direct marketing, mail order using Marks or other marks without regard to location
Retain some/all online retail profits and distribute to area developers only at discretion
If franchisee fails/refuses to offer an Authorized Sport, franchisor or designee may offer it in network area with no compensation
item12.franchisorCompeteNote: Can solicit franchisee's customers, use all alternative channels, and fill sport gaps in territory
item12.encroachmentNote: Protected for i9 Sports brand franchise but heavy reserved channel and sport-specific carveouts create real competitive risk

components.threshold: not in bottom 25% of area developers monthly
not in bottom 25% of area developers on rolling 12-month in-season surveys

minimumPerformanceStandard.multiPartNote: Mixes revenue, relative ranking, and customer satisfaction — unusual performance control system

liquidatedDamages.tenYear: average monthly royalty from prior 12 months x lesser of 24 months or remaining term
liquidatedDamages.fiveYear: remaining months x $500 monthly franchise fee

item17.unresolvedNote: Full Item 17 summary table not surfaced in this pass

item19.operatingProfitDefinition: Revenue - COGS - Total Operating Expense - Royalty Fee
item19.keyInsight: Revenue scales 7x from 1-venue ($139K) to 6+-venue ($965K) operators. Venues are the primary economic driver, not geography.

item20.unresolvedReason: Item 20 outlet tables not surfaced in file search. TOC confirms Item 20 at page 57. System considers franchise 'open' after Business Launch Date.

item21.financialConditionNote: Front-end risk disclosure says financial condition 'calls into question' ability to provide services and support
item21.financialStrengthNote: Net income is strong ($11.3M) but heavy distributions ($9.85M/year) and prior negative equity create the flagged concern. Both the risk disclosure and raw financials should be preserved.

sponsorshipProgram.nikeKidsCampsNote: Opt-in Nike Kids Camps Powered by i9 Sports through affiliate USSC, separate Program Agreement required

universalRules: Area developer / venue-sourcing model is not a storefront — investment table and operating model differ fundamentally
10-year lump-sum vs 5-year monthly franchise fee are two deal structures in one FDD — must support both
Royalty + national fund + CSC + tech fee + local ad spend are ALL greater-of formulas with delayed minimums — cannot flatten
Territory is protected network area with heavy reserved channel and sport-specific carveouts — not simple exclusive/non-exclusive
Minimum performance standard mixes revenue threshold + relative scorecard ranking + NPS ranking — multi-part object
Item 19 segmented by venue count is a teaching example for non-standard FPR structures
Operating profit definition with explicit exclusion list belongs in FPR metadata
Liquidated damages formula differs by agreement type — must preserve both versions
Centralized billing where franchisor invoices customers and deducts fees before remitting is a major control point
Financial condition concern flagged in front matter + strong net income + heavy distributions = preserve both signals
Mandatory Insurance Program with per-player pricing is sport/youth-program-specific — different from standard business insurance
Two readiness milestones (Business Launch Date vs Service Start Date) need separate tracking
Control exhibits (auto-payment, phone assignment, venue contracts, guaranty) are contract-critical objects
